title: 2026 World Cup results, standings and schedule: Live scores, group stage updates and how to watch
original_text:
2026 World Cup results, standings and schedule: Live scores, group stage updates and how to watch
The 2026 World Cup is underway across the United States, Canada and Mexico. During December's World Cup draw, the 42 qualified teams were placed into their respective group, with six additional spots filled by playoff winners.
The United States, as a co-host, was automatically slotted into Group D. It opened its tournament June 12 with a win over Paraguay. The Americans play Australia on Friday before finishing the group stage against Türkiye.
Advertisement
Canada is in Group B and made a statement in its second match, crushing Qatar 6-0 after opening with a draw against Bosnia and Herzegovina. The Canadians will finish the group stage against Switzerland.
World Cup 2026 daily schedule | Team schedules | Group tables
Mexico began the tournament with a 2-0 win over South Africa at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City and followed it up with a 1-0 victory over South Korea. The co-hosts still have Czechia remaining in Group A

Qualified teams for the 2026 World Cup
AFC: Australia, Iran, Japan, Jordan, South Korea, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Uzbekistan, Iraq CAF: Algeria, Cabo Verde, Ivory Coast, Egypt, Ghana, Morocco, Senegal, South Africa, Tunisia, DR Congo
CONCACAF: United States, Canada, Mexico, Curaçao, Haiti, Panama
CONMEBOL: Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, Uruguay
OFC: New Zealand
UEFA: England, France, Croatia, Norway, Portugal, Germany, Netherlands, Austria, Belgium, Scotland, Spain, Switzerland, Sweden, Türkiye, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia
Advertisement
World Cup 2026 format
The 48 teams were drawn in 12 four-team groups. Each team will play three games within its group to determine the countries that will advance to the knockout round stage.
There will be 32 teams advancing to the knockout round following the group stage. The top two teams from each group will advance along with the eight best third-place teams.
According to FIFA, here is how any ties will be broken:
Step 1: greatest number of points obtained in the group matches between the teams concerned; superior goal difference resulting from the group matches between the teams concerned; greatest number of goals scored in all group matches between the teams concerned
If no decision can be made through this procedure, the criteria below shall apply as follows to the two or more teams still equal on points:
Step 2: superior goal difference in all group matches; greatest number of goals scored in all group matches; highest team conduct score (players and team officials) relating to the number of yellow and red cards obtained
If no decision can be made through the procedures of step one and two above, the following shall apply:
Step 3: the two or more teams still equal on points shall be ranked according to the most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca‑Cola Men's World Ranking
As with all World Cups, teams from the same confederation (example: Brazil and Uruguay) were unable to be drawn into the same group, except for those from Europe.
